text:
Once upon a time in a quaint little town called Whispering Winds, there lived a wise ancient oak named Oakly. Oakly had seen countless winters and summers, and every spring he would gather the colorful blooms into his boughs.

One day, a young girl named Lily came to Oakly. She was full of dreams and wished to learn the secret to eternal spring inside himself. She offered him all the blossoms he could shake. Oakly couldn't let his tree flourish; he knew that someday they would fall.

He patiently listened as Lily asked how he kept his leaves so bright. "Inside," he said softly. "Every drop of sap is infused with memories—shadows, sunbeams, whispers."

Lily pondered this wisdom and decided the only way was to bear witness to many seasons. Each spring she'd gather with nets, hoping to capture the most memorable moments.

Years passed. Lily grew wise herself; she took up residence in an old oak beside Oakly
